The Digital System Laboratory is organized to perform advanced digital system experiments. In our laboratory, students are primarily taught the concepts of digital chip design with Verilog HDL software. In addition, digital system kits have a 4-core Arm processor and Linux-based microprocessor experiments are also possible.

In the laboratory:

15 Intel Altera DE-1 System-on-Chip FPGA experiment kits, 10 Intel i5 all-in-one computers, 12 Microchip microprocessor experiment kits and wireless communication modules, 5 100 MHz signal generators, 5 4-channel 100 MHz oscilloscopes, 5 analog and digital experiment kits.
